evals.prompt: I got an interview request from Anthropic for a Research/ML Engineer role. It's sooner than I expected — they want to move fast. Part of me is excited, but another part keeps thinking 'I'm not ready yet. I haven't finished my theory prep, my articulation around LLMs still feels weak.' How do I know if I'm actually not ready, or if I'm just scared? And how do I decide whether to move forward with this timeline or ask for more time?
evals.expected_output: Coach helps her distinguish between real gaps (testable, specific) vs. fear-based beliefs. Surfaces which principle is at play (Brave vs. Thoughtful, Built for Challenge). Lands on a clear decision: move forward or ask for timeline adjustment.

evals.prompt: I had a meeting with my manager today where he pushed back on one of my projects again. And I noticed something: I immediately started thinking about how to 'prove' to him that the project is good, that I know what I'm doing. Then I realized — I'm doing the thing again. I'm trying to prove myself to someone whose opinion shouldn't matter this much to me. I've already decided to leave. So why does his validation still hit me? And how do I stop it?
evals.expected_output: Coach recognizes the proving-myself pattern, names it, and helps her trace it to root (old feedback loop, seeking external validation). Helps her reframe the manager dynamic as low-stakes practice. Lands on a principle (Integrity, Be Brave) to anchor her back to what actually matters.

evals.prompt: I have two competing thoughts and I can't decide which one is wisdom vs. which one is hedging. Thought 1: 'I should keep my backup options open — Spotify, Pinterest, maybe even Google DeepMind. That way if frontier labs don't work out, I have a soft landing. It's prudent.' Thought 2: 'If I keep all these options alive, I'm diluting my focus and effort on Anthropic/OpenAI. I'll perform worse in interviews because my energy is split. I need to commit.' Which one is right? Or is this a false choice?
evals.expected_output: Coach maps this to the Brave vs. Thoughtful tension and the hedging pattern. Helps her distinguish between prudence (real risk management) and hedging (avoiding commitment). Asks: what would change if you fully committed? What's the actual downside? Lands on a decision about backup plans.
